title: viola rostrata var. phelpsiae
intro:
Viola rostrata var. phelpsiae, also known as Phelps' pansy violet, is a rare and unique variety of the Viola rostrata plant, a member of the violet family (Violaceae), characterized by its distinctive purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Viola rostrata var. phelpsiae include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
